summary: Full e2e frontend test of the new Spanish landing/funnel/Thank-You at /sp and the admin Spanish tab. Spanish UI, funnel submission (lead stored with source_page='sp'), admin Spanish hooks edit+save+reflect+restore all work. English regression at / and language toggling between / and /sp work. ISSUE: admin Spanish 'Visits' stat shows 0 despite multiple successful POST /api/track/click 200 responses to /sp (lead count works, visit/click count for source_page='sp' does not).

critical.issue: Spanish stats 'clicks' (visits) returns 0 even though POST /api/track/click is sent and returns 200 every time /sp loads (verified 4+ times with same date range 'Today'). Stats card shows 0 visits, 1 lead, 0% conversion, but By-Campaign row shows 0 visits / 1 lead / 100% — internally inconsistent. Conversion % calc likely divides by clicks; missing visit counts inflate row-level conversion and zero out top-card conversion.

integration_issues.issue: After visiting /sp multiple times the admin Spanish 'spanish-stat-clicks' card stays at 0. This is a backend/stat-counting bug, but it surfaces in the admin UI integration as broken metrics for /sp visits.

action_items: Investigate /api/admin/spanish stats aggregation: POST /api/track/click with source_page='sp' is not being counted into the Spanish 'Visits' card. Likely the source_page filter on the clicks aggregation doesn't match, or clicks are being recorded under the wrong source_page (e.g. defaulting to 'home'). Verify Mongo collection: check clicks for landing_path='/sp' or source_page='sp' for today, and confirm admin/spanish stats query filters on source_page='sp'.
critical_code_review_comments: Landing.jsx submits /track/click on mount but does NOT depend on pageLang/sourcePage in its useEffect deps (eslint-disabled). If the same SPA session loads / first then navigates to /sp, sourcePage in the closure could go stale — but our test reloaded /sp directly so this is not the failure cause here.

AdminSpanish stats and By-Campaign conversion math are inconsistent (top card uses leads/clicks*100 → 0%, table row shows 100%). Define a single canonical formula and handle clicks=0 with leads>0 case explicitly.

i18n strings file is fine; consider extracting Spanish 'errors.addrUnverified' wording shown when SmartyStreets rejects an address — current behavior caused initial QA to be stuck (acceptable production behavior, just a QA note).
seed_data_creation: Submitted 1 Spanish-funnel lead: first=PruebaQA last=Tester, year 2023, address 200 N Spring St Los Angeles CA 90012, phone (213) 555-0147, email spqa.prueba.830706@gmail.com, source_page='sp'. Spanish hooks were temporarily edited then restored to canonical originals: hook1='¿Atrapado con un Auto Defectuoso? Podría Tener Derecho a una Compensación.', hook2='Averigüe en 60 segundos si su vehículo defectuoso califica para un reembolso, reemplazo o compensación en efectivo — sin costo alguno para usted.'
context_for_next_testing_agent: Spanish UI is fully functional. The only outstanding defect is that admin Spanish 'Visits' stat does not increment when /sp is loaded (POST /api/track/click returns 200 but the aggregator doesn't surface them under source_page='sp'). Note: /api/leads does correctly tag and surface source_page='sp' (Leads card and breakdown tables show lead count). Also note: addresses like '123 Test St' get rejected by SmartyStreets address verification — use a real address (we used LA City Hall: 200 N Spring St). Emails on @example.com get rejected by deliverability; use @gmail.com etc.
rca of the issue: Reproduction: 1) Load /sp 4 times (each POST /api/track/click → 200). 2) Submit Spanish funnel (lead is created, source_page='sp'). 3) Login /admin → Spanish tab, range=Today → spanish-stat-clicks=0, spanish-stat-leads=1, spanish-stat-conv=0%. Expected: clicks>=4. Likely RCA: the click-aggregation query in GET /api/admin/spanish either (a) filters on landing_path != '/sp' (uses '/' or similar), or (b) filters on source_page='sp' but the /track/click handler is persisting source_page as 'home' or empty for /sp visits, or (c) is time-zone bounded such that 'today' excludes those entries. Frontend payload IS correct: Landing.jsx posts source_page: 'sp' for the /sp route. Mitigation: inspect the backend admin/spanish stats handler and the clicks collection to confirm where source_page='sp' rows are or are not landing.
